title: opt pass is optimizing my code away
body:
Hi.

The latest yosys from git optimizes my fsm a little too much... :smile: 

I'm a total beginner in Verilog so I'm not sure if it's just my code. I uploaded it to github
https://github.com/dvc94ch/fsm-verilog/blob/master/fsm.v

Any help would be greatly appreciated.

Below is the output in .blif and the build script that causes it:

```
read_verilog fsm.v
show -pause
proc
show -pause
opt_clean
check
show -pause
opt
show -pause
```

```
# Generated by Yosys 0.5+385 (git sha1 ddf3e2d, clang 3.7.0 -march=x86-64 -mtune=generic -O2 -fstack-protector-strong -fPIC -Os)

.model fsm
.inputs clk a b c
.outputs k m l
.names $false
.names $true
1
.names $undef
.names $false k
1 1
.names $false l
1 1
.names $false m
1 1
.end
```
code_blocks:
# Generated by Yosys 0.5+385 (git sha1 ddf3e2d, clang 3.7.0 -march=x86-64 -mtune=generic -O2 -fstack-protector-strong -fPIC -Os)

.model fsm
.inputs clk a b c
.outputs k m l

1

1 1
.names $false l
1 1

.end
